Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup

3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP); TS 38.401; NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: The purpose of the UE Context Setup procedure is to establish the UE Context including, among others, SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration. The procedure uses UE-associated signalling.
This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility).

The gNB-CU prepares and sends an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message to the candidate gNB-DU, and the candidate gNB-DU responds with an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message. This message exchange is handled on the gNB-CU for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
Feature_Objectives: To establish the UE Context at the candidate gNB-DU for an Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM-specific information, such as CSI resource configuration, LTM configuration ID mapping, and lower layer configurations, at the candidate gNB-DU.
To enable the gNB-DU to generate and provide lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell to the gNB-CU.
To facilitate early synchronization (early TA acquisition) at the candidate gNB-DU for LTM.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration for an Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell.
The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message contains one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM.
The gNB-CU may provide the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE to the candidate gNB-DU(s).
The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s).

The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

If the LTM Indicator IE set to "true" is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE.

If the LTM Indicator IE set to "C-LTM" is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall consider that the request concerns conditional LTM for the included SpCell ID IE.

If the LTM Indicator IE is set to "C-LTM" and the Request for L1 Execution Condition IE is present in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall generate the conditional LTM L1 execution condition(s) for the candidate cell(s).

If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE set to "true" is contained within the Reference Configuration IE, the gNB-DU shall provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
If the Reference Configuration Information IE is contained within the Reference Configuration IE, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

If the CSI Resource Configuration is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration(s) in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell.

If the Request for CSI-RS Resource Configuration for L1 measurements IE is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall include the CSI-RS Resource Configuration for L1 measurements IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.

If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation), in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes the Requested Target Cell ID IE.

If the Early UL Sync Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated early UL sync information from the accepted candidate cell in the gNB-DU.

If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the candidate gNB-DU.

If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE set to "complete" is contained in the LTM Configuration IE, the gNB-CU shall consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU initiates the UE Context Setup procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU.

The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message includes LTM-specific information such as LTM Information Setup IE, LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE, SpCell ID IE, CSI Resource Configuration, Reference Configuration IE, and Request for L1 Execution Condition IE.
If the LTM Indicator IE within the LTM Information Setup IE is set to "true" or "C-LTM", the gNB-DU shall consider the request concerns LTM or conditional LTM for the indicated SpCell ID IE.
If the Request for L1 Execution Condition IE is present, the gNB-DU shall generate conditional LTM L1 execution conditions.
If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE is set to "true" within the Reference Configuration IE, the gNB-DU shall prepare to provide the lower layer configuration.
The gNB-DU shall use the CSI Resource Configuration to generate LTM CSI reporting configurations.
If requested, the gNB-DU shall prepare to include CSI-RS Resource Configuration for L1 measurements IE in the response.
If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included, the gNB-DU shall prepare to include Early Sync Information IE in the response for early TA acquisition.
If the gNB-DU successfully establishes the UE context and accepts the LTM configuration, it replies to the gNB-CU with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes the Requested Target Cell ID IE, LTM Configuration IE, and Early Sync Information IE.

The LTM Configuration IE in the response contains the generated lower layer RRC configurations, L1 Execution Condition List IE, Reference Configuration Information IE, CSI-RS Resource Configuration for L1 measurements IE, and Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE.
The Early Sync Information IE in the response contains the Early UL Sync Configuration IE and potentially Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE.

Code_Instructions.description: Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message. This structure is crucial for defining the format and content of the message sent from gNB-CU to gNB-DU, including IEs like candidate cell ID, CSI resource configuration, LTM Configuration ID Mapping List, and PRACH resources, as required for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.

A helper function responsible for generating the RRC-level information and populating the necessary fields for the UE Context Setup Request. This function would prepare the content of the F1AP message based on the gNB-CU's decision to initiate LTM handover, before it is passed to the F1AP layer for encoding.

This function likely serves as an interface or encoding helper, taking the RRC-generated UE Context Setup Request information and formatting/encoding it into the F1AP message structure for transmission. It bridges the RRC/MAC layers with the F1AP protocol layer for the request message.

Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message. This structure is essential for parsing the response received by the gNB-CU from the gNB-DU, which may contain information like the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE.

This function is a helper for processing or generating the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message. On the DU side, it would populate the response. On the CU side, it would parse the received F1AP response message, extract relevant information, and pass it up to higher layers for handling the LTM handover.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
UEContextSetupRequestIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= {
 { ID id-gNB-CU-UE-F1AP-ID      CRITICALITY reject TYPE GNB-CU-UE-F1AP-ID         PRESENCE mandatory }|
 { ID id-gNB-DU-UE-F1AP-ID      CRITICALITY ignore TYPE GNB-DU-UE-F1AP-ID         PRESENCE optional  }|
 { ID id-SpCell-ID        CRITICALITY reject TYPE NRCGI            PRESENCE mandatory }|
 { ID id-ServCellIndex       CRITICALITY reject TYPE ServCellIndex          PRESENCE mandatory }|
 { ID id-SpCellULConfigured      CRITICALITY ignore TYPE CellULConfigured         PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-CUtoDURRCInformation     CRITICALITY reject TYPE CUtoDURRCInformation        PRESENCE mandatory}|
 { ID id-Candidate-SpCell-List     CRITICALITY ignore TYPE Candidate-SpCell-List       PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-DRXCycle        CRITICALITY ignore TYPE DRXCycle           PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-ResourceCoordinationTransferContainer CRITICALITY ignore TYPE ResourceCoordinationTransferContainer  PRESENCE optional  }|
 { ID id-SCell-ToBeSetup-List     CRITICALITY ignore TYPE SCell-ToBeSetup-List        PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-SRBs-ToBeSetup-List      CRITICALITY reject TYPE SRBs-ToBeSetup-List        PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-DRBs-ToBeSetup-List      CRITICALITY reject TYPE DRBs-ToBeSetup-List        PRESENCE optional }|

 { ID id-ServingPLMN        CRITICALITY ignore TYPE PLMN-Identity          PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-GNB-DU-UE-AMBR-UL      CRITICALITY ignore TYPE BitRate           PRESENCE conditional }|
 -- The above IE shall be present only if the DRB to Be Setup List IE is present.
 { ID id-RRCDeliveryStatusRequest    CRITICALITY ignore TYPE RRCDeliveryStatusRequest      PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-ResourceCoordinationTransferInformation CRITICALITY ignore TYPE ResourceCoordinationTransferInformation PRESENCE optio nal }|
 { ID id-ServingCellMO       CRITICALITY ignore TYPE ServingCellMO          PRESENCE optional }|

 { ID id-NRUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate  CRITICALITY ignore TYPE NRUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate   PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-LTEUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate CRITICALITY ignore TYPE LTEUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate  PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-PC5LinkAMBR        CRITICALITY ignore TYPE BitRate           PRESENCE optional}|
 { ID id-SLDRBs-ToBeSetup-List     CRITICALITY reject TYPE SLDRBs-ToBeSetup-List       PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-ConditionalInterDUMobilityInformation CRITICALITY reject TYPE ConditionalInterDUMobilityInformation  PRESENCE optional}|

 { ID id-LTEA2XServicesAuthorized    CRITICALITY ignore TYPE LTEA2XServicesAuthorized        PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-NRUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrateForA2X  CRITICALITY ignore TYPE NRUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate  PRESENCE optional }|
 { ID id-LTEUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrateForA2X CRITICALITY ignore TYPE LTEUESidelinkAggregateMaximumBitrate  PRESENCE optional }|

LTMInformation-Setup ::= SEQUENCE {
 lTMIndicator      LTMIndicator,
 referenceConfiguration    ReferenceConfiguration        OPTIONAL,
 cSIResourceConfiguration   CSIResourceConfiguration       OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions  ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMInformation-Setup-ExtIEs} } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

id-RequestforL1ExecutionCondition     ProtocolIE-ID ::= 904
id-LTMInformationSCGAdd        ProtocolIE-ID ::= 905
id-LTMInformationSCGMod        ProtocolIE-ID ::= 906

LTMConfiguration ::= SEQUENCE {
 sSBInformation     SSBInformation,
 referenceConfigurationInformation  ReferenceConfigurationInformation   OPTIONAL,
 completeCandidateConfigurationIndicator  CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator     OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfig    LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfigSUL   LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions      ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMConfiguration-ExtIEs } } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: For LTM handover, the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message can include RRC information. Specifically, the CellGroupConfig IE (an RRC message) can be transparently signaled within the CU to DU RRC Information IE (an F1AP IE) for LTM CSI reporting configuration. The HandoverPreparationInformation IE (an RRC message) can also be included in the CU to DU RRC Information IE for handover scenarios.
